name: plan_manifest_wave2_surface_wiring
description: Expose manifest-id based data-plane operations through MCP and CLI so registry-first execution is actually usable.

jobs.label: wire_data_surfaces_to_registry_first_inputs
jobs.prompt:
## Goal
Make the CLI and MCP data surfaces hyper-usable for registry-backed plan/approval manifests.

## Read first
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/artifacts/workflow/_control_manifest_registry_contract.json`
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/data_plane.py`
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/mcp/tools/data.py`
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/cli/commands/data.py`
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/mcp/cli_metadata.py`
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/unit/test_cli_data_surface.py`
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/unit/test_mcp_docs_and_metadata.py`

## Required changes
1. Update the MCP `praxis_data` tool so it attempts to obtain a Postgres connection from subsystems and passes it into the data-plane runtime when available.
2. Add first-class params for `plan_manifest_id` and `approval_manifest_id` to the tool schema/docs/examples.
3. Update the CLI `workflow data ...` help and payload assembly so manifest-id based approve/apply flows are explicit and ergonomic.
4. Keep offline behavior graceful for non-registry operations.
5. Do not make unrelated docs changes.

## Required tests
Update/add tests covering:
- MCP tool forwards manifest ids and pg connection into execution
- CLI help text mentions manifest-id lanes
- metadata/docs examples include the new params without regressing old ones

Touch only the files in scope. Do not modify API handlers in this packet.

Database: `postgresql://nate@127.0.0.1:5432/dag_workflow`
